summary: Backend-only verification completed for test-layout cleanup and prompt-test modernization scope. Full Vitest discovery passes after canonical moves, targeted prompt/contract suites pass, and no regression found in active automated tests.
action_items: Optional cleanup later: keep Python harnesses under tests/helpers/legacy quarantined; they are stale by design (old fixed test counts/path assumptions) but no longer affect active discovery.
If legacy helpers are revived, update them to yarn + current tests/** paths and current suite count (793).
critical_code_review_comments: Vitest discovery remains correctly scoped to tests/**/*.test.ts and tests/**/*.test.tsx via /app/vitest.config.ts include rules.
Strong contract suites stayed intact after path updates: procedure, registry, how-to-check, route wiring, no-hidden-authority, and strictness all pass.
Prompt-related suites in tests/prompts pass under current contracts; modernization did not reduce strict behavioral assertions in critical guardrails.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Validated requested cleanup outcomes: no active root-level *.test.* files at /app root depth, active suites run from /app/tests, legacy Python harnesses are under /app/tests/helpers/legacy. This run used existing MOCKED OpenAI/auth/storage/context-engine boundaries inside relevant Vitest suites as intended.
rca of the issue: No defect reproduced in this scope. Regression risk from test relocation was mitigated by full-suite run with zero failures and stable discovery patterns.
